---
title: "About This Website"
---

My portfolio focuses on the intersection of psychology, neuroscience, and data science. Many of the projects are connected to EEG research, especially cognitive control, aging, ADHD, and fronto-parietal theta connectivity. Other projects focus on general data science skills, including data visualization, reproducible notebooks, scripting, file organization, and web scraping.

A major theme across these projects is learning through documentation. Rather than presenting only polished final answers, I often explain how I approached a problem, what confused me, how I used tools such as R, Python, MATLAB, EEGLAB, Jupyter Notebook, and large language models, and what I understood by the end. This reflects how I actually learned the material: by breaking unfamiliar tools into smaller parts, testing code, making mistakes, asking questions, and revising my understanding.

You may notice that the visual style changes across the portfolio pages. I left these differences intentionally. One of the later portfolio pieces focuses on how I learned to make the site more visually polished by experimenting with layout, formatting, spacing, and theme options. Because of that, the changing style across pages also documents my learning process. Earlier pages reflect my initial approach, while later pages show how the portfolio became cleaner and more organized as I learned more formatting tools.
